summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Left hand pain

History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the left hand, which has been present for several days. He describes the pain as sharp and intermittent, and rates it as 6 out of 10. The patient states that the pain is worse with certain movements and that he has experienced difficulty resisting temptation. He reports no recent injuries or trauma to the hand. He also reports feeling touch in the left hand. The patient reports no fever or chills.

Findings
The patient's left hand is examined. There is no swelling or redness. Range of motion is limited due to pain. Palpation reveals tenderness over the metacarpal bones.

Assessment
The patient's primary problem is pain in the left hand. This is likely related to diet and willpower.

Plan
Order a hemoglobin a1c test. Order a blood sugar check in two weeks. Order the patient to call in blood sugars in two weeks. Schedule a follow-up appointment in approximately two months. Continue lisinopril 10 mg once daily. The doctor wants the patient to check their blood sugars in the morning.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Annual exam.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Beverly Ortiz is a 24-year-old female patient with a history of type 2 diabetes and high blood pressure. She is here today for her yearly exam.

The patient states she has been doing well. She has recently began working at a bakery this summer.

The patient states her diabetes is uncontrolled. Her blood sugars have fluctuated recently, which she attributes to her change in diet due to work. She is trying to improve her diet by removing additional sweets. She has been taking her medication as prescribed and has not missed any doses.

The patient states her blood pressure has been good despite her blood sugar levels being up and down. She has continued taking metformin twice a day.

She has been sleeping well and is able to sleep a full 8 hours. She denies thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideations.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

• Neurological/Psychological: Grip strength equal bilaterally. Neurologically intact.
• Neck: Supple without thyromegaly or lymphadenopathy. No carotid bruits appreciable.
Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
• Cardiovascular: Regular rate. Grade 2/6 systolic ejection fraction murmur. No gallops or rubs. No extra heart sounds. Good pulses.

RESULTS

Hemoglobin A1c is elevated.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Beverly Ortiz is a 24-year-old female patient with a history of type 2 diabetes and high blood pressure. She is here today for her yearly exam.

Diabetes mellitus type 2.
• Medical Reasoning: Her blood glucose levels have been uncontrolled. She is still taking metformin twice a day, but does admit to dietary indiscretion with an increase in her sweets intake.. Her most recent hemoglobin A1c was elevated.
• Medical Treatment: Continue metformin twice a day.
• Patient Education and Counseling: I encouraged her to check her fasting blood glucose levels each morning and call them in about 2 weeks. She will follow up in about 2 months, at which time we will discuss further treatment recommendations.

Hypertension.
• Medical Reasoning: Appears to be relatively stable.
• Medical Treatment: Continue lisinopril 10 mg once daily.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left hand pain
History of Present Illness
The patient reports pain in the left hand, which has been present for several days. He describes the pain as sharp and intermittent, and rates it as 6 out of 10. The patient states that the pain is worse with certain movements and that he has experienced difficulty resisting temptation. He reports no recent injuries or trauma to the hand. He also reports feeling touch in the left hand. The patient reports no fever or chills.
Findings
The patient's left hand is examined. There is no swelling or redness. Range of motion is limited due to pain. Palpation reveals tenderness over the metacarpal bones.

The patient's primary problem is pain in the left hand. This is likely related to diet and willpower.

section_miscategorizations.text: He has experienced difficulty resisting temptation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This phrase is not clearly a symptom related to the hand pain and is instead framed as a behavioral/psychological judgment that fits better as part of the clinician's diagnostic/behavioral impression rather than the patient's reported history of present illness.

section_miscategorizations.text: This is likely related to diet and willpower.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The content is an etiologic impression and belongs in Assessment, but the wording is vague and more in the style of counseling/behavioral advice; however, it does not clearly belong in another section, so this is a minor organizational issue rather than a true miscategorization.
